sanskrit:
आख्याहि मे को भवानुग्ररूपो नमोऽस्तु ते देववर प्रसीद।
विज्ञातुमिच्छामि भवन्तमाद्यं न हि प्रजानामि तव प्रवृत्तिम्॥
transliteration: ākhyāhi me ko bhavānugra-rūpo namo ’stu te deva-vara prasīda | vijñātum icchāmi bhavantam ādyaṃ na hi prajānāmi tava pravṛttim ||
literal_meaning: Tell me who You are, of fierce form; obeisances to You, best of gods, be gracious. I desire to know You, the First; for I do not understand Your purpose.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna moves from awe to active inquiry: he admits perceptual overload, names his ignorance, and requests relationship—an ego-to-Self dialogue that transforms terror into curiosity.
psychological_state: Acute awe-to-curiosity shift: dissolving defensive grandiosity, tolerating not-knowing, and seeking relational meaning with an overwhelming inner presence.
trigger_scenario: After a panic attack or psychedelic glimpse of vastness, you stammer, 'What was that? I don’t understand it, but I need to relate to it rather than run.'
anchor_text: Ask the fierce Unknown to reveal itself and guide you.
